description:
You are studying the evolutionary conservation of Cytochrome c across diverse eukaryotic taxa. You have been given a FASTA file containing 8 protein sequences at ~/UGENE_Data/evolution/cytochrome_c_diverse.fasta.

Perform the following multi-step analysis in UGENE:

1. Open the FASTA file in UGENE and load the sequences into the alignment viewer.
2. Perform a multiple sequence alignment using the MUSCLE algorithm.
3. Configure the alignment's consensus sequence calculation to use a strict 100% threshold (identities only). At this threshold, any alignment column that is not 100% identical across all 8 sequences will be represented by an ambiguity character or gap.
4. Export the resulting 100% consensus sequence in FASTA format to ~/UGENE_Data/evolution/results/strict_consensus.fasta
5. Analyze the exported consensus sequence to identify universally conserved blocks (continuous stretches of standard amino acids without any ambiguity characters).
6. Create a plain text report at ~/UGENE_Data/evolution/results/conservation_report.txt containing the following information (use clear labels):
   - The total length of the alignment (number of columns)
   - The total number of completely conserved positions (count of standard amino acids in the strict consensus sequence)
   - The single longest continuous conserved amino acid motif (the actual sequence string, e.g., 'VQKCAQCHTV')
   - The length of this longest conserved motif

Ensure both the FASTA file and the text report are saved exactly at the specified paths.
